source.title: The role of replay and theta sequences in mediating hippocampal-prefrontal interactions for memory and cognition

confidence_rationale: Jadhav lab review in Hippocampus; explicitly frames hippocampal-prefrontal interactions via replay and theta sequences as the mechanism for context-dependent memory retrieval and decision-making. Directly addresses the cue-differentiation requirement by arguing that PFC-hippocampal theta interactions at different learning stages mediate different retrieval modes. Good source quality; high mapping fidelity.
failure_signatures: The review is primarily phenomenological -- the causal roles of replay vs. theta sequences in guiding PFC activity are proposals awaiting closed-loop intervention evidence.
The framework relies on the assumption that replay content is read out by PFC, but the mechanism for this readout is not established.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Hippocampal-prefrontal interactions mediated by replay (SWRs) and theta sequences play complementary roles at different learning stages: encoding and retrieval via replay, deliberative decision making and future-path planning via theta; PFC reads hippocampal sequences to guide actions.
mapping.ree_translation: This is the mechanistic bridge between Miller & Cohen 2001 (PFC bias signals) and ARC-007 (hippocampal trajectory retrieval): PFC does not store trajectories but reads and selects among hippocampal sequences via theta-coupled query. The cue-differentiation requirement (Topic 3) is satisfied by the finding that different PFC activity states (encoding vs. retrieval phase) engage hippocampal replay in different ways.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The 'PFC reads hippocampal sequences' formulation is inferred from coordination -- the actual synaptic projection from hippocampus to PFC that allows this readout is structural, but the functional gating by PFC cue content is not directly demonstrated.
mapping.source_context: Review paper in Hippocampus journal; Jadhav lab (Brandeis); covers both awake SWR replay and theta-sequence mechanisms in spatial memory tasks.

confidence_components.notes: Best available review connecting the two bodies of evidence (PFC control + hippocampal sequences) into a unified framework.
